incidents.victim: Government agencies and enterprises worldwide (200+ organizations)
incidents.vector: DNS hijacking of 18,000+ end-of-life SOHO routers (Mikrotik, TP-Link) to intercept Microsoft Office OAuth authentication tokens without deploying malware.
incidents.impact: Attackers quietly siphoned authentication tokens from 200+ organizations and 5,000+ devices, bypassing MFA by capturing post-authentication tokens. No malware needed — purely DNS manipulation.

incidents.vector: Exploitation of zero-day vulnerability CVE-2026-35616 (severity 9.1) in FortiClient EMS allowing unauthenticated remote code execution via crafted requests.
incidents.impact: In-the-wild exploitation began March 31 over Easter weekend. CISA ordered federal agencies to patch by Thursday. Second FortiClient EMS vulnerability in three weeks.

incidents.vector: TeamPCP repurposed existing cloud-worm infrastructure to deploy a wiper payload that activates when target systems match Iran's timezone or have Farsi as default language.
incidents.impact: Wiper destroys data on local machines and, if Kubernetes access exists, every node in the cluster. Financially motivated group injecting itself into the Iran war.

incidents.vector: Data-wiping attack by Handala hacktivist group linked to Iran's MOIS, claiming retaliation for the Feb 28 missile strike on an Iranian school.
incidents.impact: Stryker reported offices in 79 countries affected, 5,000+ workers sent home in Ireland, and order processing, manufacturing and shipping disrupted. Company declared fully operational again by Apr 2 after three weeks of recovery.

incidents.vector: Large-scale automated exploitation of React2Shell (CVE-2025-55182) in Next.js applications to gain initial access, followed by multi-phase credential harvesting via NEXUS Listener C2 framework.
incidents.impact: At least 766 hosts compromised across multiple cloud providers. Stolen credentials include database passwords (91.5% of victims), SSH keys (78.2%), AWS credentials (25.6%), Stripe API keys, and GitHub tokens.

incidents.vector: Compromise of npm account of lead Axios maintainer via social engineering (fake video call), followed by publication of malicious versions injecting a cross-platform RAT via a trojanized dependency.
incidents.impact: Malicious axios@1.14.1 and axios@0.30.4 were live for hours, potentially affecting ~600,000 downloads. RAT scrapes SSH keys, cloud credentials, and crypto wallets. Google attributed the attack to North Korean group UNC1069.

incidents.vector: Unauthorized network access discovered March 28; attack method not publicly disclosed.
incidents.impact: Hasbro activated business continuity plans, took systems offline, and filed with the SEC. Remediation expected to take weeks with significant operational disruption.

incidents.vector: TeamPCP exploited access gained via the Trivy supply chain compromise to steal an Amazon API key, then exfiltrated ~92 GB of data from the Commission's Europa.eu AWS platform.
incidents.impact: Data from 42 internal clients and 29 EU entities potentially stolen, including ~52,000 email communication files. Stolen data appeared on ShinyHunters dark web marketplace on March 28.

incidents.vector: Active exploitation of CVE-2025-53521, an unauthenticated remote code execution vulnerability in F5 BIG-IP Access Policy Manager, initially miscategorized and later recategorized as RCE.
incidents.impact: F5 confirmed active exploitation. CISA added to KEV catalog. NCSC urged UK organizations to investigate for compromise on all affected products regardless of patch status.

incidents.vector: Exploitation of CVE-2026-3055 (severity 9.3) in Citrix NetScaler ADC/Gateway enabling unauthenticated memory disclosure — reminiscent of prior CitrixBleed vulnerabilities.
incidents.impact: CISA ordered federal agencies to patch immediately. Researchers reported exploitation within days of disclosure. Third major CitrixBleed-type vulnerability, widely used in enterprise authentication.

incidents.victim: Organizations using Trivy vulnerability scanner (1,000+ SaaS environments)
incidents.vector: TeamPCP compromised Aqua Security's Trivy open-source vulnerability scanner via a GitHub Actions misconfiguration, injecting credential-stealing malware into official releases.
incidents.impact: Over 1,000 SaaS environments confirmed impacted with ongoing downstream compromises expected. Mandiant warned of widespread extortion campaigns as stolen credentials are weaponized by collaborating threat groups.

incidents.vector: Exploitation of CVE-2026-3502 in TrueConf video conferencing software's update mechanism to distribute malicious updates to dozens of government agencies via a compromised on-premises server.
incidents.impact: Attackers deployed Havoc penetration testing tool and ShadowPad malware across government endpoints. CISA gave federal agencies two weeks to patch. TrueConf is used by 100,000+ organizations globally, often in air-gapped environments.

incidents.victim: Diplomatic organizations and critical infrastructure (Middle East)
incidents.vector: GreenGolf (Iran MOIS) conducting cyberespionage with new Rust-based malware variants LampoRAT and BlackBeard, plus UDP-based backdoors and AI-assisted development techniques.
incidents.impact: Expanded targeting of diplomatic organizations, energy, maritime, and financial infrastructure across the Middle East. Shift to Rust likely aims to evade endpoint detection and achieve longer-term persistence.

incidents.vector: Qilin ransomware group attacked Die Linke's IT network at the end of March, encrypting systems and stealing employee records.
incidents.impact: The party shut down its entire IT network. Employee records were stolen but member data was reportedly not compromised. Qilin claimed responsibility.

incidents.vector: French threat actor used Anthropic's Claude AI agent with compromised credentials to bypass security measures and upload a backdoored update to the BuddyBoss platform.
incidents.impact: Almost 250 websites compromised, mostly American universities located in the Middle East. First documented case of an AI coding agent being weaponized for a supply chain attack.

incidents.vector: Unauthorized access to internal information-sharing systems first detected Feb 28; access route has since been blocked.
incidents.impact: Customer, partner, and employee personal data confirmed stolen. Core procurement, production, and shipping systems were unaffected and business operations continue normally.

incidents.victim: IoT device owners worldwide (3M+ devices across 4 botnets)
incidents.vector: DOJ, with Canadian and German authorities, dismantled infrastructure behind four botnets (Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid, Mossad) that compromised routers and web cameras for DDoS-for-extortion operations.
incidents.impact: Over 3 million IoT devices were compromised. Aisuru alone launched 200,000+ attack commands. DDoD attacks targeted U.S. Department of Defense infrastructure. Multiple domain seizures executed.

incidents.vector: Spearphishing led to developer workstation compromise, then a malicious pod was deployed to steal a high-privileged Kubernetes service account token, enabling lateral movement into cloud financial infrastructure.
incidents.impact: Millions in cryptocurrency stolen. Attack mirrors techniques used in the $1.5B Bybit heist. Overly permissive Kubernetes RBAC permissions allowed the attacker to pivot from a single pod to full cloud-level financial system access.

incidents.victim: Russia — FSB targets messaging app users globally
incidents.vector: Russia-based actors targeting high-risk individuals' WhatsApp, Signal, and Messenger accounts through phishing, QR code attacks, and attempts to link unauthorized devices.
incidents.impact: NCSC and international partners issued joint advisory warning government officials and individuals with access to sensitive information. Builds on earlier campaigns by FSB-linked actors targeting messaging platforms.
